title: The one money task today
subtitle: A single-action practice for clearing a money procrastination item in five minutes or less.
excerpt: A short activity from Day 45 of the MONEY program. Pick one money task you have been putting off. Do it now, not perfectly, just done.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 45 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A short activity from Day 45 of the MONEY program. Procrastination around money tasks is rarely about time. It is about the low-grade dread that makes a task feel bigger than it is. This practice makes the task smaller by doing it first.
Write down, in one sentence, the single money task you have been putting off the longest. Not a project, one action. An email. A phone call. An invoice. A price update. One thing.

Now ask: what is the smallest version of this task I can do in the next five minutes? Not the whole thing, just enough that it is started or sent or recorded. Draft the email without sending it if needed. Set up the invoice without the final number. Begin.
Set a timer for five minutes. Do only that task. When the timer goes, you are done, finished, filed, or as far as it needed to go today.
Say out loud: "I did the thing I was avoiding. I am someone who moves on money." Say it even if it feels dramatic. Especially if it does.
Adapted from Day 45 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
